mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) and low unemployment (UNRATE) signal robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of PAYEMS > 136k and UNRATE < 7% fails to produce positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts (HOUST) and robust retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong demand-side momentum, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of HOUST > 880 and RSAFS > 432k fails to produce positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 230 (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank intervention through rate hikes, which increases equity discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL > 230 fails to produce negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of cases, or the signal's Rank IC is below -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) during a supply shock regime indicates reduced corporate output, which correlates with negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to produce negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of supply shock cases, or the signal's Rank IC is below -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates sustained economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to produce positive 60-day returns in 60%+ of cases, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS < 0.1%) signal accommodative monetary policy, which reduces borrowing costs and stimulates equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS < 0.1% fails to produce positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of cases, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a favorable macroeconomic environment for equity markets, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of PAYEMS > 136k and FEDFUNDS < 0.1% fails to produce positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).
